name: M1911A1 Pistol
prompt:
Vietnam War era Colt M1911A1 semi-automatic pistol. Standard US officer/NCO sidearm since 1911.

Physical structure (~0.22m total length):
- Frame: steel lower frame with grip area
  - Trigger: curved trigger in trigger guard
  - Grip: checkered grip panels (walnut or black plastic)
  - Magazine well: at base of grip
  - Grip safety: at rear of grip (beaver tail)
  - Manual safety: thumb safety on left side
- Slide: steel upper slide, reciprocating
  - Rear sight: simple notch
  - Front sight: small blade
  - Ejection port: on right side top
  - Serrations: diagonal grip lines at rear of slide
- Barrel: short ~5-inch barrel, visible at muzzle
  - Barrel bushing: cylindrical at muzzle end
- Hammer: external hammer at rear, cocked or down
- Magazine: 7-round single-stack magazine (hidden inside grip)

Colors: Parkerized gray-green (0x444840) or blued (0x2a2a30), dark grips (0x2a2016), silver controls (0x666666)
Orientation: Lying on right side, barrel along +Z
Scale: Real-world meters (~0.22m long, ~0.14m tall)
